candidates.title: Better Together – Partnering Up for Hong Kong Movies
candidates.blurb: Exhibition mapping Hong Kong cinema's collaborative ecosystems—directors, producers, stars—at the site where the industry's literal infrastructure once stood.
candidates.why_shortlisted: Hong Kong cinema history in situ

candidates.title: Anne Imhof
candidates.blurb: Turner Prize–winning artist's first Hong Kong institutional solo, likely adapting her durational performance work to a former colonial police compound.
candidates.why_shortlisted: Major European artist in charged colonial space

candidates.title: Alicja Kwade: Waiting Pavilions
candidates.blurb: Kwade's mirror-and-stone pavilions reflect and distort the Victorian prison yard, proposing parallel realities within the architecture of confinement.

candidates.title: Treasures of Global Jewellery from The Metropolitan Museum of Art: The Body Transformed
candidates.blurb: Met jewellery spanning Africa, Europe, Asia and the Americas shown in a museum devoted to imperial Chinese art—framing ornament as global language.
candidates.why_shortlisted: Global Met collection in Sinocentric context

candidates.title: Ancient Egypt Unveiled: Treasures from Egyptian Museums
candidates.blurb: Cairo loans positioning HKPM as a Belt and Road cultural node, testing whether imperial China and pharaonic Egypt read as parallel civilisational brands.

candidates.title: The Hong Kong Jockey Club Series: The Forbidden City and the World: Cultural Encounters
candidates.blurb: Long-term display tracing Qing court diplomacy and trade—essentially Hong Kong's own historical business model installed in a museum founded to affirm continuity.
candidates.why_shortlisted: Hong Kong's function as curatorial subject

candidates.title: Joyful Encounters: The Connoisseurship and Art of Wong Kwan Shut
candidates.blurb: Hong Kong painter-collector Wong Kwan Shut's holdings and practice shown at scale, documenting mid-century literati aesthetics sustained through the city's industrialisation.

candidates.title: A History of China in Silk: The Chris Hall Collection at the Hong Kong Palace Museum
candidates.blurb: Private British collection of Chinese textiles installed at a museum devoted to reunification—colonial knowledge systems re-housed by the inheritor state.

candidates.title: Fermata: Hong Kong in Venice Collateral Event of the 61st International Art Exhibition – La Biennale di Venezia
candidates.blurb: Venice Biennale collateral restaged in Tsim Sha Tsui, re-contextualising whatever Hong Kong sent to represent itself to Europe for local reinterpretation.

candidates.title: Blooming: The Art of Gardens in East and West
candidates.blurb: Survey of garden representation across cultures, likely foregrounding Chinese scholar gardens versus European landscape tradition—nature as constructed idea.

candidates.title: Proudly from Canton: The Muwen Tang Collection of Cantonese and Export Art
candidates.blurb: Export porcelain, reverse-glass paintings, and trade-era Canton work—Hong Kong's immediate cultural predecessor shown as hybrid production for external markets.
candidates.why_shortlisted: Canton export trade as Hong Kong DNA

candidates.title: Wu Guanzhong Art Sponsorship Thematic Exhibition Series: Wu Guanzhong: Encountering Landscapes
candidates.blurb: Wu Guanzhong's synthesis of Chinese ink and Western abstraction presented as resolved aesthetic—state-approved modernism via Hong Kong's permanent collection.

candidates.title: Dreamchasers: Stories of Hong Kong Art
candidates.blurb: Permanent display charting local artists' careers and practices—essentially the museum's attempt to codify what Hong Kong art is and has been.
